## CONSULTATION HANDOFF

```
change_type: Add Feature
feature_name: distribution-exe-npm
date: 2026-05-15
advisor: [@devstarter-techlead, @devstarter-pm]
```

---

### Section 1 — Problem / Request

**What the user described:**
```
Need to make this toolkit as EXE — users cannot see source code.
Distribution should be professional and hide/protect the markdown files.
```

**Change type detected:**
- [x] Add Feature — new distribution mechanism that doesn't exist yet

---

### Section 2 — Analysis Summary

**Situation understood:**
```
DevStarter v3.8.0 distributes via raw bash script (install.sh) which
installs .md files to ~/.claude/. Files are fully readable on disk.
User wants professional distribution that hides source from casual view.

Core architectural constraint: Claude Code skill system requires
readable .md files on disk at runtime. True binary compilation
would break Claude Code integration. Partial protection only is possible
while keeping current architecture.
```

**Root cause / Gap:**
```
No professional distribution channel exists.
Files are directly readable in ~/.claude/ after install.
No Windows-native installer (only bash script).
No npm package for cross-platform distribution.
```

**Affected area / files (if identified):**
```
- scripts/install.sh — existing bash installer (keep, extend)
- scripts/ — new: build-installer.ps1 / build-npm.sh
- package.json — new: npm package definition
- installer/ — new: NSIS or Inno Setup config for Windows EXE
- .github/workflows/ — extend CI to build and sign artifacts
- README.md — update install instructions
```

---

### Section 3 — Recommended Approach

**Recommended option from consultation:**
```
Option A + C combined:
  A — Self-Extracting Installer EXE (Windows, via NSIS or Inno Setup)
  C — npm package (npx devstarter init) for Mac/Linux
```

**Why this was recommended:**
```
Option A: Professional Windows distribution — signed EXE, license check
possible, files not casually browsable pre-install.

Option C: Standard cross-platform distribution via npm — version
management, auto-update via npm, npx one-liner for Mac/Linux users.

Together they cover all platforms with professional distribution while
preserving full Claude Code integration (files still land in ~/.claude/
and are read by Claude Code at runtime — unavoidable given architecture).

NOT Option B (standalone CLI rewrite) — would lose Claude Code
integration entirely. NOT Option D (license gate) unless monetizing.
```

**Estimated effort:** M (2–8h per platform)

**Risks or caveats:**
```
- Files still readable after install (inherent architectural constraint)
- NSIS/Inno Setup requires Windows build environment in CI
- npm package must handle ~/.claude/ path correctly on all platforms
- Code signing (Windows EXE) requires certificate ($99-$400/yr)
- npx approach requires Node.js installed on target machine
```

---

### Section 4 — Acceptance Criteria

**Done when (Windows EXE):**
```
Given DevStarter is not installed on Windows
When  user double-clicks DevStarter-Setup-v3.8.0.exe
Then  all skills/agents/sdlc files install to C:\Users\X\.claude\
      with progress UI, no terminal required
```

**Done when (npm):**
```
Given Node.js is installed on Mac/Linux
When  user runs: npx devstarter init
Then  all files install to ~/.claude/ correctly
      and /devstarter-menu works in next Claude Code session
```

**Regression guard (must still work):**
```
- install.sh bash path still works (existing users unaffected)
- All 28 existing commands work post-install via new distribution methods
- update.sh migration logic still applies
```

---

Type:        Add Feature
Feature:     Distribution — Windows EXE installer + npm package
Advisor:     @devstarter-techlead, @devstarter-pm
Date:        2026-05-15

Problem:
  DevStarter only distributes via raw bash script. Users can see all
  source files in ~/.claude/ after install. No professional distribution
  channel. No Windows-native installer.

Root cause / Gap:
  No installer EXE (Windows) and no npm package (cross-platform).
  Architecture requires .md files on disk for Claude Code to read,
  so full source hiding is not possible — professional installer is the
  pragmatic solution.

Recommended:
  Option A — Windows self-extracting EXE (NSIS or Inno Setup) — Effort: M
  Option C — npm package (npx devstarter init) — Effort: M

Acceptance criteria:
  Given Windows + no install → When run EXE → Then files in ~/.claude (GUI)
  Given Mac/Linux + Node.js → When npx devstarter init → Then files in ~/.claude

Saved to: memory/consult-2026-05-15-improve-new-features.md

  "approve"        → launch /devstarter-change (skip intake, go to analysis)
  "revise [notes]" → update any section above
